title: Ask For Help

content.statement: Progress accelerates when you seek guidance from people who have already solved the problem you are facing.
content.question: Who has already solved the problem you are trying to figure out alone?
content.equation: Learning Speed = Access to Experience × Question Quality × Implementation
content.mental_model: Borrowing a map, someone else has already walked the terrain.
content.business_lens: Pay for advice, mentorship, coaching, or advisory access when it reduces costly mistakes.
content.life_lens: Do not confuse independence with refusing support.
sop: Identify the specific problem you need solved
Find someone who has solved it
Ask for low-friction access, such as email, a call, or a review
content.explainer: Asking for Help is a leverage move, not a weakness. The right advisor can help you avoid expensive mistakes, see obvious gaps, and move with more confidence. The value is not just information. It is compressed experience.
questions: Who has already solved the problem you are trying to figure out alone?
What question have you been avoiding asking because it might expose what you don't know?
The last time someone gave you advice, did you actually implement it within a week?
content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Access to Experience 7, Question Quality 5, Implementation 8. Score = 7 times 5 times 8 = 280 out of a possible 1,000. Higher means advice actually compresses your timeline. Lower means you're collecting opinions without acting on any of them.
content.closing_statement: Borrowed experience is faster than earned scars.

caption:
Principle Nº 268: Ask For Help

Progress accelerates when you seek guidance from people who have already solved the problem you are facing. Asking for Help is a leverage move, not a weakness.

Ask yourself: Who has already solved the problem you are trying to figure out alone?
